response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced Staff/Senior Software Engineer with 15+ years of professional development experience, making him severely overqualified for an entry-level position. While he technically lists Java as a skill and has a relevant CS degree, his primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and Java appears marginal. More critically, his German is self-described as 'Basic,' which disqualifies him from a client-facing consulting role in Germany that explicitly requires strong German communication. There is also no insurance domain experience.
strengths: Strong computer science foundation with a relevant CS degree and 15+ years of hands-on development across multiple stacks
Demonstrated cloud and agile experience (AWS CDK, TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) matching several technical requirements
Broad OOP background including Java in skills list, plus prior Java use at Altom for robotic arm projects
concerns: Severe overqualification — currently a Staff Software Engineer; applying to an entry-level role signals either role mismatch or undisclosed motivations that may affect retention
German proficiency listed as 'Basic,' which is insufficient for a client-facing consulting role that explicitly requires strong verbal and written German
Java is a peripheral skill; the candidate's primary experience is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — Java is not a core competency despite the role centering on it
